evals.name: swift-testing-simple
evals.prompt: What's the difference between `#expect()` and `try #require()` in Swift Testing?

assertions.text: Explains #expect is soft check (continues on failure)
Explains #require halts test on failure
Shows #require returns unwrapped value

evals.name: swift-testing-medium
evals.prompt: I want to test the same login function with 10 different username/password combinations. What's the cleanest way in Swift Testing?

assertions.text: Recommends @Test with arguments parameter
Shows parameterized test syntax with tuple or custom type
Mentions .tags for filtering test subsets
Warns about argument count limits or shows zip() for multiple collections

evals.name: swift-testing-complex

assertions.text: Identifies XCTAssert in @Test silently passes (no XCTestCase context)
Explains confirmation() counts when CLOSURE RETURNS, not when callback fires
Shows fix: use #expect for assertions, withCheckedContinuation for callbacks
Mentions this is anti-pattern C5 or references framework mixing danger

evals.name: xctest-migration-simple
evals.prompt: How do I convert `XCTAssertEqual(a, b)` to Swift Testing?

assertions.text: Shows #expect(a == b) syntax
Mentions Swift Testing provides better failure messages with expression context

evals.name: xctest-migration-medium
evals.prompt: I have an XCTest file with setUp(), tearDown(), and XCTestExpectation waits. How do I migrate it to Swift Testing?

assertions.text: setUp -> stored property initialization or init
tearDown -> deinit (but warns tearDown is not exactly equivalent)
XCTestExpectation -> confirmation() or async/await
Warns about framework mixing danger during migration

evals.name: xctest-migration-complex

assertions.text: Identifies test prefix + @Test causes double execution (XCTest discovers via prefix, Swift Testing via @Test)
Explains XCTest parallel ran in SEPARATE PROCESSES, Swift Testing runs in ONE PROCESS
Fix for double: remove test prefix or remove @Test
Fix for races: eliminate static state or @Suite(.serialized)

evals.name: async-testing-simple

assertions.text: Identifies wait(for:) blocks thread in async context causing deadlock
Shows fix: await fulfillment(of:) instead of wait(for:)

evals.name: async-testing-medium
evals.prompt: My test for `isLoading == true` is flaky - sometimes passes, sometimes fails. The ViewModel calls `await repository.fetchItems()`. How do I reliably test the loading state?

assertions.text: Identifies race condition: async completes before assertion runs
Recommends withMainSerialExecutor from swift-concurrency-extras
Explains Task.yield() inside mock for deterministic suspension
Does NOT recommend Task.sleep as primary solution

evals.name: async-testing-complex

assertions.text: Explains internal Task is fire-and-forget, test can't await it
Option: use expectation + sink on @Published with dropFirst()
Option: withMainSerialExecutor to serialize Task execution
Mentions refactoring option: inject Task or make loadData() async
Warns about dropFirst() - subscribe BEFORE calling loadData()

evals.name: observable-testing-simple
evals.prompt: How do I test an @Observable ViewModel's state changes in Swift Testing?

assertions.text: For sync changes: direct assertion works
For async changes: withObservationTracking or confirmation
Test type needs @MainActor when ViewModel is @MainActor

evals.name: observable-testing-medium

assertions.text: Add @MainActor to the test struct/class, not just the method
Shows @Suite @MainActor struct CartViewModelTests syntax
Alternative: await MainActor.run {} as workaround

evals.name: observable-testing-complex

assertions.text: Uses withObservationTracking in a loop or multiple times
Explains willSet semantics - tracking sees value BEFORE change
Alternative: confirmation(expectedCount: 3) with observation
Addresses timing: mock should yield to allow state assertions

evals.name: tca-testing-simple
evals.prompt: What's a TestStore in TCA and why should I use it?

assertions.text: TestStore tests reducers by sending actions and asserting state changes
Provides exhaustive assertion - must assert ALL state changes and effects
Allows dependency overriding for isolation

evals.name: tca-testing-medium

assertions.text: Explains exhaustive model: must receive() all effect actions
Shows await store.receive(.loginResponse) syntax
Uses case key path: .receive(\.loginResponse)
Alternative: store.exhaustivity = .off for partial testing

evals.name: tca-testing-complex

assertions.text: Use TestClock and advance(by:) for deterministic time
Override dependency: withDependencies { $0.continuousClock = testClock }
TCA + Swift Testing silent pass: requires TCA >= 1.12
Shows testClock.advance(by: .seconds(3)) to trigger 3 ticks

evals.name: ui-testing-simple
evals.prompt: How do I wait for a button to appear in XCUITest?

assertions.text: Uses waitForExistence(timeout:)
Shows button.waitForExistence(timeout: 5) syntax

evals.name: ui-testing-medium
evals.prompt: My XCUITest is flaky because a system alert for location permission blocks the flow. How do I handle system alerts?

assertions.text: Use addUIInterruptionMonitor for system alerts
Must tap on app element after adding monitor to trigger it
Alternative: reset authorization in setUp via launchArguments

evals.name: ui-testing-complex
evals.prompt: Our XCUITest suite has 200 tests and takes 45 minutes. Tests are brittle - small UI changes break many tests. How do we improve maintainability and speed?

assertions.text: Page Object Model - encapsulate element queries in screen objects
Use accessibility identifiers, not text matching
Run in parallel with Test Plans
Reduce UI tests - push logic to unit tests (test pyramid)
Launch arguments to skip animations, pre-seed state

evals.name: snapshot-testing-simple
evals.prompt: How do I set up snapshot testing for SwiftUI views?

assertions.text: Use PointFree's swift-snapshot-testing library
assertSnapshot(of: view, as: .image)
First run records reference, subsequent runs compare

evals.name: snapshot-testing-medium
evals.prompt: My snapshot tests pass locally but fail on CI with tiny pixel differences. How do I fix this?

assertions.text: Pin to specific device/size: .image(layout: .device(config: .iPhone13))
Set precision parameter: .image(precision: 0.99)
Record snapshots on CI, not locally (or vice versa, pick one)

evals.name: snapshot-testing-complex

assertions.text: assertSnapshot uses XCTAssert under the hood - same C5/C7 mixing issue
Keep snapshot tests as XCTestCase until library supports Swift Testing
For dark mode: use parameterized test or named strategies
Shows .image(traits: .init(userInterfaceStyle: .dark))

evals.name: integration-testing-simple
evals.prompt: How do I test network code without hitting real servers?

assertions.text: Use URLProtocol subclass to intercept requests
Or inject mock URLSession/protocol dependency

evals.name: integration-testing-medium
evals.prompt: My Core Data tests are slow and sometimes fail with 'store already in use'. How do I isolate Core Data in tests?

assertions.text: Use in-memory store: NSInMemoryStoreType or /dev/null URL
Create fresh container per test
Use NSPersistentStoreDescription with url = URL(fileURLWithPath: "/dev/null")

evals.name: integration-testing-complex
evals.prompt: Our app stores auth tokens in Keychain. Tests randomly fail because Keychain state persists between test runs. We also have UserDefaults and SwiftData. How do we fully isolate each test?

assertions.text: Keychain: protocol wrapper + in-memory mock, or unique service name per test
UserDefaults: suiteName creates isolated domain, delete in tearDown
SwiftData: in-memory ModelConfiguration
Keychain state persists across app installs on simulator - may need manual clear
Use dependency injection for all storage, not direct access

evals.name: anti-patterns-simple

assertions.text: Date() makes test non-repeatable (F.I.R.S.T violation)
May fail at certain times (midnight, DST, etc.)
Fix: inject deterministic date

evals.name: anti-patterns-medium

assertions.text: No assertion - test with no #expect always passes
This is anti-pattern C2: no assertion test
Coverage executed != correctness verified

evals.name: anti-patterns-complex

assertions.text: static var sut - shared state between tests (H1)
wait(for:) in async context - deadlock (C1)
Thread.sleep - artificial delay (H3)
UUID() - non-deterministic (H5)
print(result) with no assertion (C2)
#expect in XCTestCase - silently ignored (C6)
sut not initialized in setUp - will crash

evals.name: test-organization-simple
evals.prompt: What's a good naming convention for test methods?

assertions.text: test_<method>_<condition>_<expected> or similar structured format
Or @Test("does X when Y") descriptive string in Swift Testing

evals.name: test-organization-medium
evals.prompt: Our test suite takes 15 minutes. How do we speed it up?

assertions.text: Run tests in parallel (Test Plans or xcodebuild -parallel-testing-enabled)
Identify slow tests and optimize or isolate them
Move integration tests to separate scheme/plan
Use test pyramid - more unit tests, fewer UI tests

evals.name: test-organization-complex
evals.prompt: We have flaky tests that randomly fail on CI but pass locally. They're blocking PRs. How do we handle them without just deleting them?

assertions.text: Quarantine flaky tests in separate Test Plan
Run quarantined tests but don't block PR on failure
Track flaky tests with tickets, time-box fixes
Investigate root causes: shared state, timing, CI environment differences
Consider @Suite(.serialized) for Swift Testing parallel issues
